name: Curator’s POV
description:
How has the most meme’able, memorable and mischievous animal known to man been represented across art history in the subcontinent?

Join curator Khushi Bansal as she explores the many lives of the Indian cat—appearing in paintings, sculptures, matchbox covers, artist sketchbooks, and beyond.

This event is part of the closing weekend celebrations for The Many Lives of the Cat.

name: Textile Conversations
description:
Image Credits: Poornima Jain-Haveli Textile Collection
Textile Conversations is designed as a two-part engagement programme exploring material knowledge in the Haveli Collection through dialogue and hands-on practice.

Through knowledge exchange between collaborators, each session positions textile researchers and practitioners as contributing distinct forms of expertise - technical, functional, embodied and aesthetic.

The programme draws strongly from the Haveli Collection at MAP, that consists of over 2741 textile objects and over 2050 archival materials, all belonging to the Haveli fashion brand established in the 1980s by Poornima Jain in Maharani Bagh, Delhi.

name: Miniature to Mischief
description:
Image Credits: Sanchita Jain
Join us for a lively conversation about cats, their histories, myths, and personalities. Exploring two books on Indian cats, B. N. Goswamy's The Indian Cat and Olliewanders, you can swap stories, challenge common misconceptions, and celebrate the surprising complexity of our feline companions.

Expect good conversation, plenty of colour, and a "mosaic of many cats" who may look suspiciously like their humans, especially for anyone who suspects they might, spiritually, be a cat.

This event is part of the closing weekend celebrations for The Many Lives of the Cat.

name: Purrfect Patchwork
description:
Create your own playful patchwork cat with artist Vasundhara W in this fun, hands-on workshop inspired by geometric shapes and fabric craft. Using tangram-style forms, colourful fabric, and simple stitching techniques, participants will design amusing cats in a variety of poses.

By the end of the session, you’ll have a fun patchwork cat placemat to take home.

This event is part of the closing weekend celebrations for The Many Lives of the Cat.

name: Small Worlds, Many Cats
description:
Image credits: Mahaveer Swami
Learn traditional miniature techniques with artist Mahaveer, using the cat as your muse while exploring line, detail, and colour.

This hands-on workshop brings together storytelling, craftsmanship, and the feline’s enduring charm in Indian art.

This event is part of the closing weekend celebrations for The Many Lives of the Cat.

name: Write from Art, Write from Story
description:
Explore the layered presence of the cat as muse, metaphor, and companion through a creative writing workshop led by writer and editor Indira Chandrasekhar.

This event is part of the closing weekend celebrations for The Many Lives of the Cat.
